name: void-dominion-mobile
description: Capacitor Android wrapper that packages the Void Dominion prototype (a self-contained HTML game) into an installable APK. Standalone — not part of the pnpm workspace.

scripts.brand: npm run assets && node patch-android.mjs && node patch-updater.mjs
scripts.apk: npm run www && npm run sync && npm run brand && cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug --no-daemon

comments.overrides: Pin the transitive node-tar to a patched line (>=7.5.16) to clear the GHSA path-traversal / symlink / file-smuggling advisories (tar <=7.5.15). We keep Capacitor 6 (the v6/JDK17 APK pipeline) instead of the breaking @capacitor/cli@8 bump. replace@1.2.2 pins minimatch 3.0.5 exactly (ReDoS: GHSA-23c5-xmqv-rm74 / GHSA-3ppc-4f35-3m26 / GHSA-7r86-cg39-jmmj; patched in 3.1.4), and xcode@3.0.1 wants uuid ^7.0.3 with no patched 7.x for GHSA-w5hq-g745-h8pq (patched in 11.1.1) — both overrides are scoped to those parents so the rest of the tree (e.g. glob's minimatch@8) is untouched.
comments.postinstall: tar 7's CJS interop leaves `tar_1.default` undefined, so @capacitor/cli@6's `tar_1.default.extract` crashes `cap add/sync`. patch-capacitor-tar.mjs rewrites it to `(tar_1.default||tar_1).extract` (works under tar 6 and 7) after install, so the override doesn't break the build.
